violations.description: General employee practices. — --Employee failed to maintain a high degree of personal cleanliness or observe proper hygienic practices. Observed employee using a cloth towel to dry their hands after washing their hands. Use handwash sink only for washing hands.

Preventing contamination during storage. — --Food item was not stored in a clean, dry location a minimum of 6 inches above the floor. Bags of rice and onions stored on the floor next to the 3 compartment sink.

Preventing contamination from equipment, utensils, and wiping cloths. — --In-use utensil was not properly stored during pauses in food preparation or dispensing. Scoop in the spice container had the handle in the the product.

General equipment and utensils - Materials and design. — --Material of nonfood contact surface was not constructed or designed to be smooth, nonabsorbent and cleanable. Bare wood used to hold up the flattop on the table is not sealed and is and uncleanable surface.

Cleaning frequency of equipment and utensils. — --Food contact surface was not cleaned as frequently as required. The inside of the microwave and the freezer needs to be cleaned. Containers holding spices on the prep table need to be cleaned.--Nonfood contact surface was not cleaned as frequently as required. Table underneath the flattop, the front and sides of the grill/oven need to be cleaned. Fronts and handles on upright cooler and freezer need cleaned.--Ventilation hood system was not cleaned properly and/or as frequently as required. -Clean filters.

Design and construction of floors, walls, and ceilings. — --Floors, walls, and/or ceiling were not constructed or designed to be smooth, nonabsorbent, and easily cleanable. Bare wood blocks under the reach-in cooler are unsealed and are not a cleanable surface. Need to be painted or sealed.

Cleaning and maintenance of floors, walls and ceilings. — --Physical facilities (e.g. floors, walls, ceiling etc…) were not clean or not cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. Floors and walls in the dish area, cook-line and prep areas all need a thorough cleaning.

General premises. — --Items unnecessary for the operation and maintenance of the food establishment were being stored on the premises. Excess equipment and personal items are being stored in the dish area and back storage areas. Continue to reduce items that are not necessary for a cooking establishment.
Design, construction, clean, maintained (1 pt deducted)
Cleaning frequency, maintained clean (2 pts deducted)
